evidences.title: Binance API for Historical Crypto Prices | by Zachary Raicik | Medium
evidences.content: symbol: The crypto symbol of the asset (e.g. ETHUSDT) interval: The interval at which we want the request to return prices (e.g. 1d, 12h) start\_date: The day at which you want the request to start returning prices end\_date: The last day at which you want the request to start returning prices The function’s s signature will look like this: ``` def get_historical_prices( symbol: str, interval: str, start_date: dt.datetime, end_date: dt.datetime,) def get_historical_prices symbol interval start_date end_date ``` The next two bits of code take the arguments passed to the function and create the information needed by the Binance API to make a request. Note that Binance require date to be expressed as UNIX timestamps. [...] parser.add_argument( "--symbol", default="ETHUSDT", help="The symbol of the cryptocurrency to retrieve price data for (e.g. 'ETHUSDT')", ) parser.add_argument( "--interval", default="1d", help="The interval at which to retrieve price data (e.g. '1d' for daily data)", ) parser.add_argument( "--start_date", type=dt.datetime.fromisoformat, help="The start date for the price data to retrieve, in YYYY-MM-DD format", ) parser.add_argument( "--end_date", type=dt.datetim...

evidences.title: BinanceSpotRest.Endpoints.MarketData.Klines.Query — Binance Spot Rest v0.2.1
evidences.content: Notes: If `startTime` and `endTime` are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Supported values for `timeZone`: Hours and minutes (e.g. `-1:00`, `05:45`) Only hours (e.g. `0`, `8`, `4`) Accepted range is strictly [-12:00 to +14:00] inclusive If `timeZone` provided, kline intervals are interpreted in that timezone instead of UTC. Note that `startTime` and `endTime` are always interpreted in UTC, regardless of `timeZone`. Data Source: Database Response: [...] # BinanceSpotRest.Endpoints.MarketData.Klines.Query (Binance Spot Rest v0.2.1) View Source Klines ### Kline/Candlestick data ``` GET / api/ v3/ klines ``` Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Weight: 2 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | interval | ENUM | YES | | startTime | LONG | NO | | endTime | LONG | NO | | timeZone | STRING | NO | Default: 0 (UTC) | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1000. | Supported kline intervals (case-sensitive): | Interval | `interval` value | --- | | seconds | `1s` | | minutes | `1m`, `3m`, `5m`, `15m`, `30m` | | hours | `1h`, `2h`, `4h`, `6h`, `8h`, `12h` | | days | `1d`, `3d` |...

evidences.title: GitHub - maxgfr/binance-historical: Get historical klines from binance api
evidences.content: ### Library ``` import{getKline, Kline} from'binance-historical'; const result Array< Kline> = await getKline('ETHUSDT', '4h', new Date('01-09-2020'), new Date('01-12-2021'),); console. log(result);// [// ...,// {// openTime: 1579953600000,// open: '159.30000000',// high: '161.00000000',// low: '158.70000000',// close: '160.41000000',// volume: '25015.67605000',// closeTime: 1579967999999,// quoteAssetVolume: '4001959.95527980',// trades: 14330,// takerBaseAssetVolume: '13000.12296000',// takerQuoteAssetVolume: '2079824.44045350',// ignored: '0'// },// ...// ] ``` ## About Get historical klines from binance api ### Topics [...] ## Installation ### Via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) ``` brew install maxgfr/tap/binance-historical ``` ### Via npm ``` npm install -g binance-historical ``` ### Download binary Download the latest binary for your platform from the releases page. ## Usage ### CLI #### Interactive mode ``` binance-historical download ``` #### Non-interactive mode Pass all options as arguments: ``` binance-historical download \ --pair BTCUSDT \ --interval 4h \ --start 2024-01-01 \ --end 2024-12-31 \ --output ./data/ \ --format json ``` Export as CSV: ``` binance-historical downlo...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] | Security Type | Description | --- | | NONE | Endpoint can be accessed freely | | TRADE | Endpoint requires sending a valid API-Key and signature | | USER\_DATA | Endpoint requires sending a valid API-Key and signature | | USER\_STREAM | Endpoint requires sending a valid API-Key | | MARKET\_DATA | Endpoint requires sending a valid API-Key | ## Authentication Timing ``` if(timestamp<(serverTime + 1000) &&(serverTime - timestamp)<= recvWindow){// process request} else{// reject request} ``` [...] Data Source: Memory ### Get Candlestick Data > Example ``` -X "GET"" ``` ``` impor...

evidences.title: Market Data Endpoints — python-binance 0.2.0 documentation
evidences.content: Fetch klines for any date range and interval ``` # fetch 1 minute klines for the last day up until now klines = client. get_historical_klines("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = client. get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = client. get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK,"1 Jan, 2017") ``` ## Get Historical Kline/Candlesticks using a generator Fetch klines using a generator ``` for kline in client. get_historical_klines_generator("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC"): print(kline) # do something with the kline ``` ## Get average price for a symbol [...] ``` agg_trades = client. aggregate_trade_iter(symbol = 'ETHBTC', start_str = '30 minutes ago UTC') # iterate over the trade iterator for trade in agg_trades: print(trade) # do something with the trade data # convert the iterator to a list# note: generators can only be iterated over once so we need to call it again agg_trades = client. aggregate_trade_iter(symbol = 'ETHBTC', '30 minutes ago UTC') agg_trad...

evidences.title: THE NATURE OF THE 1-MINUTE CHART
evidences.content: Article # THE NATURE OF THE 1-MINUTE CHART Daily Clever News Nov 11, 2025 Follow View Hidden Replies Report Block User Analyzing the 1-minute chart (1m chart) is an essential skill for scalpers — traders who operate on short-term moves, entering and exiting positions within a few minutes to a few tens of minutes. To master this timeframe, you need to understand its nature, use the right tools, and maintain absolute discipline in every decision. $BTC $BNB $ETH UNDERSTANDING THE NATURE OF THE 1M CHART The 1-minute chart reflects the smallest and fastest price movements. Each candle represents one minute of trading, so signals appear constantly and are highly noisy. This timeframe is best used to find precise entry and exit points, not to predict long-term trends. [...] CONCLUSION The 1-minute chart is not for predicting the future — it’s for observing how price reacts in real time. It demands focus, reflexes, and iron discipline. Once you understand the rhythm of each candle and the flow of capital within this tiny window, you’ll stop chasing the market — and start moving in sync with it. Functioning as a permissionless, decentralized Validity-Rollup, often referred to as a "ZK-Rollup," StarkNet serves as an L2 network atop Ethereum. This setup allows decentralized applications (dApps) to scale their computations while maintaining Ethereum's composability and security. StarkNet's efficiency and safety are underpinned by its use of the STARK cryptographic proof system, renowned for its scalability and robustness.
